1	---
2	ddx:
3	  id: FEAT-008
4	  depends_on:
5	    - helix.prd
6	    - FEAT-002
7	    - FEAT-014
8	---
9	# Feature: DDx Server Web UI
10	
11	**ID:** FEAT-008
12	**Status:** In Progress
13	**Priority:** P1
14	**Owner:** DDx Team
15	
16	## Overview
17	
18	The DDx server (`ddx-server`) serves a web UI for browsing artifacts, beads,
19	the artifact dependency graph, and DDx run activity. Artifacts include markdown
20	documents plus sidecar-identified media such as diagrams, wireframes, images,
21	and PDFs. The UI is a SvelteKit application built with Svelte 5, embedded into
22	the Go binary via `embed.FS`, and served alongside the MCP and HTTP API
23	endpoints from a single process.
24	
25	`ddx-server` runs as a per-user host daemon (one instance per machine, see
26	FEAT-002 and FEAT-020), so the same UI enumerates every project that the
27	host+user registry knows about and binds its navigation to one selected
28	project context at a time. The node-aware URL structure and combined
29	cross-project dashboards are specified in FEAT-021; FEAT-008 owns the
30	underlying views that those routes render.
31	
32	The UI consumes GraphQL operations defined in `cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql`.
33	Every page lists its required query or subscription by name for traceability.
34	
35	## Problem Statement
36	
37	**Current situation:** DDx artifacts, beads, and the artifact graph are only accessible via CLI commands or MCP tool calls. There's no visual way to browse the artifact library, see the dependency graph, check bead status, regenerate generated artifacts, or review layer-aware run evidence.
38	
39	**Pain points:**
40	- CLI output for `ddx doc graph` is text-only — dependency graphs need visual rendering
41	- Bead status and dependency trees are hard to grok in terminal output
42	- DDx run activity has no browsable interface that preserves the `work` -> `try` -> `run` layers
43	- Non-markdown artifacts have no type-aware browser preview, even when the graph knows their identity
44	- Generated artifacts have no UI affordance for checking provenance or requesting regeneration
45	- New team members can't quickly understand what artifacts exist or how they relate
46	
47	**Desired outcome:** A web dashboard served from the same `ddx-server` binary that already handles MCP and HTTP API. Developers open a browser, see their artifact library, dependency graph, bead board, generated-artifact provenance, and DDx run activity — all from one URL.
48	
49	## Architecture
50	
51	### Single Binary, Multiple Surfaces
52	
53	```
54	ddx-server binary
55	├── /            → Web UI (embedded SvelteKit)
56	├── /api/        → HTTP REST API (JSON) — frozen
57	└── /graphql     → GraphQL endpoint (gqlgen)
58	```
59	
60	The SvelteKit frontend queries the `/graphql` endpoint for all data. REST endpoints
61	remain unchanged and are consumed by the CLI and MCP tools only.
62	
63	### Project Context
64	
65	The UI is rendered under the node-scoped URL structure defined in FEAT-021:
66	`/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/...` for project-scoped views and
67	`/nodes/:nodeId/...` for combined node-wide dashboards. The root `/`
68	redirects to `/nodes/:nodeId` using the ID returned by `GET /api/node`
69	(FEAT-020). When more than one project is registered the navigation bar
70	exposes a project picker populated from `GET /api/projects`; selecting a
71	project swaps the `:projectId` segment in place. All GraphQL queries issued by the
72	UI are bound to the selected project context.
73	
74	### Project Navigation Tabs
75	
76	Each `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/...` page exposes the following
77	project-scoped tabs in the global navigation, in this order:
78	
79	| Tab | Route segment | Purpose |
80	|---|---|---|
81	| Overview | (none) | Project home, recent activity, operator-prompt input (ADR-021) |
82	| Beads | `beads` | Bead list / kanban / ready / detail (US-082*) |
83	| Artifacts | `artifacts` | Artifact browser (US-080, US-081b, US-083) |
84	| Graph | `graph` | Document dependency graph (US-081, US-081c) |
85	| Runs | `runs` | Layer-aware run history with `work` / `try` / `run` chips, row expansion into the shared `work` / `try` / `run` run-detail tab strip, and re-queue (US-086b, US-086c) |
86	| Workers | `workers` | Live + recent worker processes for this project (US-085b, US-086, US-086a) |
87	| Personas | `personas` | Persona browser + role bindings (US-097) |
88	| Plugins | `plugins` | Plugin registry + install/uninstall (US-098) |
89	| Commits | `commits` | Project git commit log (US-093, FEAT-021) |
90	| Efficacy | `efficacy` | Model efficacy + comparisons (US-096) |
91	
92	**Sessions and Executions are retired as top-level activity tabs**
93	(Story 8). The unified Runs tab carries the shared layer-aware tab
94	structure and inline row expansion that surface what those tabs used to
95	show. The shared detail tabs are:
96	
97	- `work` rows: `overview`
98	- `try` rows: `overview`, `prompt`, `response`, `tools`, `evidence`
99	- `run` rows: `overview`, `prompt`, `response`, `session`, `tools`, `evidence`
100	
101	- `layer=run` rows expand to AgentSession transcript / billing / cached
102	  tokens / stderr (the old Sessions detail).
103	- `layer=try` rows expand to the `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` bundle
104	  metadata, check results, and verdict (the old Executions detail).
105	- `layer=work` rows expand to queue inputs and child run links.
106	
107	**URL deprecation policy.** During the deprecation window, the legacy
108	URLs respond with **302 redirects (NOT 301)** to the matching filtered
109	Runs URL with all query params preserved, plus a `Sunset` response
110	header that names the cutoff date for the redirects:
111	
112	| Legacy URL | Redirect target |
113	|---|---|
114	| `/sessions` | `/runs?layer=run` |
115	| `/sessions#<id>` | `/runs?layer=run&session=<id>` |
116	| `/executions[?bead=&verdict=&harness=&q=&after=]` | `/runs?layer=try` with the original query string preserved verbatim |
117	| `/executions/[id]` | `/runs/[runId]?bundle=true` |
118	
119	302 (not 301) is intentional: bookmarks and external link aggregators
120	should not cache the redirect permanently while we still own the legacy
121	routes. The redirect response carries `Sunset: <RFC 9745 date>` and the
122	project nav drops the Sessions/Executions entries once the redirects and
123	the internal-link sweep land (`shellRoutes.ts`, `BeadDetail.svelte`,
124	commits page, agent page, Workers detail).
125	
126	### Build Pipeline
127	
128	```
129	cli/internal/server/frontend/   → SvelteKit + TypeScript
130	  bun run build                 → cli/internal/server/frontend/build/
131	cli/internal/server/            → Go with embed.FS
132	  go build                      → ddx-server (single binary with embedded UI)
133	```
134	
135	During development, SvelteKit's dev server proxies `/graphql` to the running Go server.
136	
137	### Technology Choices
138	
139	| Layer | Choice | Why |
140	|-------|--------|-----|
141	| Frontend framework | **Svelte 5** | Compile-time optimizations, no Virtual DOM |
142	| Build tool | **SvelteKit** | Router, layout, API routes, hooks, adapter-static for embedding |
143	| Styling | **Tailwind CSS** | Consistent with website (Hextra uses Tailwind) |
144	| Data fetching | **graphql-request + graphql-ws** | Lightweight GraphQL client with typed queries and WebSocket subscriptions |
145	| Graph visualization | **D3.js** or **Cytoscape.js** | For document dependency graph rendering |
146	| Embedding | **Go embed.FS** | Compile frontend into the binary — no external files needed |
147	| Go HTTP | **Chi** or **net/http** | Standard library compatible routing |
148	| Go GraphQL | **gqlgen** | Schema-first, code-generated resolvers |
149	
150	### Alternatives Considered
151	
152	**Templ + htmx** — simpler (no JS toolchain), but the dependency graph visualization and interactive bead board need more interactivity than htmx provides well. Templ + htmx is great for CRUD; we need a richer graph/dashboard experience.
153	
154	**Local-first with CRDT sync** — interesting for offline use but overkill for v1. The UI is primarily read-heavy (browsing docs, viewing graphs). Can be added later with PowerSync or ElectricSQL if needed.
155	
156	## Requirements
157	
158	### Functional
159	
160	1. **Artifact browser, viewer, editor, and regeneration**
161	   - List artifacts by category (prompts, personas, patterns, templates, ADRs,
162	     SDs, diagrams, wireframes, generated images, PDFs)
163	   - Full-text search across textual artifact content, sidecar metadata, and
164	     markdown frontmatter
165	   - View markdown artifacts rendered as markdown with syntax highlighting
166	   - Render artifacts by `media_type`:
167	     - `text/markdown`: markdown renderer with syntax-highlighted code blocks
168	     - `image/svg+xml` with Mermaid provenance: sanitized inline SVG preview
169	     - `application/vnd.excalidraw+json`: Excalidraw read-only embed
170	     - `image/*`: inline image preview with metadata fallback
171	     - `application/pdf`: embedded PDF preview with open-file fallback
172	     - unknown or binary media: metadata panel plus open/download affordance
173	   - Show `ddx:` frontmatter or sidecar `.ddx.yaml` metadata (id,
174	     `media_type`, dependencies, `generated_by`, and staleness status)
175	   - Clickable dependency links — navigate upstream/downstream docs inline
176	   - **Editor**: in-browser markdown editing with save-to-disk (calls API to write file)
177	   - Sidebar showing document dependency tree for current doc
178	   - Staleness indicator per document (fresh/stale/missing-deps)
179	   - Generated-artifact provenance indicator: show producing run id, prompt or
180	     generator summary, and source-hash status when `generated_by` is present
181	   - Generated-artifact staleness is artifact-local: a `generated_by.source_hash`
182	     mismatch marks only the generated artifact stale and does not cascade like
183	     `depends_on`
184	   - Regenerate action for generated artifacts calls the `artifactRegenerate`
185	     mutation, returns the new run id, and links directly to the produced run
186	     detail and updated artifact
187	
188	2. **Document dependency graph**
189	   - Interactive visual graph (D3/Cytoscape) showing all document relationships
190	   - Color-code nodes by staleness (green=fresh, orange=stale, red=missing-deps)
191	   - Click node to navigate to document detail
192	   - Filter by document type, staleness status
193	   - Zoom/pan for large graphs
194	   - Layout options (hierarchical top-down, force-directed)
195	
196	3. **Bead views**
197	
198	   The beads UI runs entirely in the browser. On load, the client fetches the
199	   full bead set from the `beads` query (GraphQL). All search, filter, sort, and graph
200	   traversal happens client-side using an in-browser data layer.
201	
202	   **Master-detail list view:**
203	   - Searchable list: full-text across title, description, acceptance, labels
204	   - Sortable columns: ID, title, status, priority, labels, owner, updated_at
205	   - Filterable by status, priority, label, owner, and a `Needs review` lane
206	   - Click a bead to open detail panel (split pane or slide-over)
207	   - Detail shows: all fields, dependency tree, execution beads, agent sessions
208	   - Graph traversal: click a dependency → navigate to that bead's detail
209	   - Drill-down into run records linked to the bead (shows layer, pass/fail,
210	     logs, duration)
211	
212	   **Kanban board view:**
213	   - Columns represent status: `open`, `in_progress`, `closed`
214	   - Cards within columns ordered by priority (P0 top, P4 bottom)
215	   - Dependency grouping: beads that block each other cluster visually
216	     (similar to Trello, Fuzz by 37signals, GitHub Projects)
217	   - Drag-and-drop to change status (calls `beadUpdate` mutation)
218	   - Swimlanes option: group rows by label (e.g., `area:cli`, `area:web`)
219	   - Color coding: priority → card border, labels → chips, blocked → dimmed
220	   - Collapsed card shows: title, priority badge, label chips, dep count
221	   - Expanded card shows: description, acceptance, execution summary
222	
223	   **Ready queue view:**
224	   - Filtered view showing only beads with all dependencies satisfied
225	   - Sorted by priority, then by creation date
226	   - One-click claim (calls `beadClaim` mutation)
227	   - Single-ticket ready work is shown ahead of epics; open epics appear in a
228	     separate epic lane rather than the ordinary execute-loop queue
229	
230	   **Bead detail actions:**
231	
232	   The detail panel is not just a viewer — it is the primary interaction
233	   surface for evaluating and driving work items.
234	
235	   *Navigation:*
236	   - `spec-id` links to the governing artifact (rendered inline or navigates
237	     to the document viewer with that artifact's content)
238	   - Parent bead link navigates to the parent's detail
239	   - Dependency links navigate to each dep's detail
240	   - Run records link to the layer-aware run detail view (logs, results)
241	   - Agent session links navigate to the agent log viewer for that session
242	
243	   *Review and re-evaluation:*
244	   - "Review" action: side-by-side view of the bead's acceptance criteria
245	     vs the latest execution results and implementation diff. The user or
246	     agent evaluates whether the work actually meets the spec. This dispatches
247	     the TD-033 structured evidence envelope in no-tool reviewer mode so the
248	     reviewer reasons only over the assembled evidence.
249	   - "Re-review" action on closed beads: re-evaluates against current
250	     artifact state — did the governing spec change since the bead was
251	     closed? If so, flag as potentially stale.
252	   - Artifact drift indicator: if the `spec-id` artifact's content hash
253	     changed since the bead was last updated, show a warning badge.
254	
255	   *Status and lifecycle:*
256	   - Re-open a closed bead (status → open) with a reason field
257	   - Re-run bead work: dispatch `ddx try <bead>` through the server worker API,
258	     then open the resulting `try` record under the layer-aware run history
259	   - Run linked checks: when the bead references an execution definition,
260	     dispatch that definition as project checks and show the resulting record
261	     separately from bead re-run history
262	   - Claim / unclaim for agent coordination
263	   - Inline status transitions (open → in_progress → closed)
264	
265	   *Mutation:*
266	   - Edit all bead fields inline (title, description, acceptance, labels,
267	     priority, spec-id)
268	   - Create new beads with guided form
269	   - Add/remove dependencies by selecting other beads
270	   - All mutations call the HTTP API which writes to the JSONL store
271	
272	4. **Agent log monitor**
273	   - Live-updating list of agent invocations (newest first)
274	   - Columns: timestamp, harness, model, tokens, duration, exit code
275	   - Click to expand: DDx metadata, native session/trace references, and any
276	     DDx-owned detail available for that invocation
277	   - Filter by harness, time range, exit code
278	   - Token usage summary (provider-native or DDx-observed, depending on source)
279	   - Auto-refresh on configurable interval (or subscription in v2)
280	
281	5. **Layer-aware run history and detail**
282	   - List DDx run records from the unified substrate with explicit layer chips:
283	     `work`, `try`, and `run`
284	   - Filter by layer, status, harness, provider, model, bead id, artifact id,
285	     and time range without introducing a run-type catalog beyond the three
286	     layers
287	   - Free-text search across bead id, bead title, and output excerpt; multi-
288	     column sort on Started / Duration / Cost. Search/sort/filter primitives
289	     are the shared Story 6 chip + sort-header + search-input components
290	     (PRIMITIVE reuse — Runs has its own page model for live updates,
291	     parent/child hierarchy, layer expansion, and re-queue affordances).
292	     Story 7's virtualization wrapper is reused with a Runs-specific live
293	     adapter when row counts exceed 1k.
294	   - Inline row expansion is **layer-aware** and opens the shared tabbed
295	     run-detail surface (replaces the retired Sessions and Executions
296	     top-level activity tabs and uses the shared `work` / `try` / `run`
297	     tab strip):
298	     - `layer=work` row expands to queue inputs, selected beads, stop
299	       condition, retry/defer decisions, and child try-attempt links in
300	       chronological order. The detail panel exposes the `overview` tab only.
301	     - `layer=try` row expands to bead id, base/result revisions, isolated
302	       worktree path, merge/preserve outcome, checks, verdict, and child
303	       layer-1 run links — sourced losslessly from the
304	       `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` bundle attached to the layer-2 record.
305	       The detail panel exposes `overview`, `prompt`, `response`, `tools`,
306	       and `evidence`.
307	     - `layer=run` row expands to prompt/config summary, power bounds,
308	       selected harness/provider/model, token + cached-token + cost +
309	       duration signals, output, stderr, and evidence links — sourced
310	       losslessly from the joined `AgentSession` row (FEAT-010 §"Layer-to-
311	       substrate mapping for the Runs UI"). The detail panel exposes
312	       `overview`, `prompt`, `response`, `session`, `tools`, and `evidence`.
313	   - Drill-down links preserve hierarchy in both directions:
314	     `work` -> `try` -> `run`, with breadcrumbs back to parent records
315	   - Artifact-producing runs show `produces_artifact`; artifact pages link back
316	     to the producing run and expose regeneration history
317	   - **Re-queue affordances** (Story 8):
318	     - `layer=try` rows show a `Re-queue` button when the originating bead is
319	       open AND no try is currently active for that bead. The button opens a
320	       confirmation dialog prefilled with the original config; the client
321	       generates the `idempotencyKey` and sends it with the
322	       `runRequeue` mutation (FEAT-010).
323	     - `layer=run` rows show `Re-queue` only when prompt / harness / model are
324	       replayable; re-queueing a successful run requires double-confirmation.
325	     - `layer=work` rows show **`Start worker from this drain`** (different
326	       label from "Re-queue"; same dispatch path) prefilled with the
327	       original queue inputs, stop condition, selected beads, and harness /
328	       profile from the original work run.
329	     - Re-queue against a closed bead is disabled and surfaces a
330	       "Reopen bead first" link.
331	     - Every successful re-queue emits a `run_requeue` audit event on the
332	       originating bead (schema: FEAT-010 §"Re-queue audit event schema").
333	   - Layer-aware run views consume read-only GraphQL/HTTP state except for the
334	     narrow `artifactRegenerate` and `runRequeue` mutations.
335	
336	6. **Status dashboard**
337	   - Summary cards: document count by type, bead counts by status, stale document count, recent agent activity
338	   - Quick links to ready beads, stale documents, recent agent activity
339	   - Project health indicators (library populated, config valid, beads active)
340	   - Worker list includes single-ticket workers and epic workers separately,
341	     showing the active bead/epic, worktree, branch, and current phase.
342	     The worker list is populated from
343	     `GET /api/projects/:project/workers` (see FEAT-002). Each worker card
344	     renders the `current_attempt.phase` field as a color-coded phase badge:
345	
346	     | Phase | Badge color |
347	     |-------|------------|
348	     | `queueing` | gray |
349	     | `launching` | blue |
350	     | `running` | green |
351	     | `post_checks` | yellow |
352	     | `landing` | purple |
353	     | `done` | teal |
354	     | `preserved` | orange |
355	     | `failed` | red |
356	
357	   - Clicking a worker card expands to show the `recent_phases` timeline from
358	     `GET /api/projects/:project/workers/:id`. The expanded view also shows
359	     elapsed time, harness/model identity, cumulative token counts, and a link
360	     to the full execution evidence bundle for completed attempts.
361	   - For in-flight workers, the card auto-refreshes using the `workerProgress`
362	     subscription. The UI updates the phase badge, elapsed time, and token count from
363	     each progress event without re-fetching the full worker record. When the terminal
364	     phase event arrives, the stream closes and the card switches to a static completed state.
365	
366	7. **Epic execution view**
367	   - Lists open epics separately from single-ticket ready work
368	   - Shows epic branch name, epic worktree path, active child bead, and merge
369	     gate status
370	   - Shows sequential child-bead history on the epic branch
371	   - Allows the user to inspect the final epic merge candidate and merge-gate
372	     results before the merge commit is created
373	
374	8. **Provider / Harness Dashboard**
375	
376	   A dedicated page — separate from the status dashboard, agent log monitor,
377	   and worker progress view — that gives operators a clear picture of
378	   configured providers and the routing signals DDx uses to make harness
379	   decisions.
380	
381	   **Provider list panel (left/top):**
382	   - One row per configured harness (claude, codex, opencode, gemini, …)
383	   - Columns: harness name, display name, availability badge (`available` |
384	     `unavailable` | `unknown`), auth state badge, quota/headroom pill
385	     (`ok` | `blocked` | `unknown`), cost class, freshness timestamp
386	   - Searchable by harness name, model, status, or cost class
387	   - Sortable by any column; default sort by availability, then name
388	   - Filter chips: availability status, auth state, quota state, signal source
389	   - All data fetched from `GET /api/providers` on load; auto-refreshes on a
390	     configurable interval (default 60 s)
391	
392	   **Provider detail panel (right/bottom, shown on row click):**
393	   - Full routing signal snapshot for the selected harness
394	   - Per-model quota/headroom with source attribution and `unknown` when no
395	     stable live source exists
396	   - Historical usage table: 7-day and 30-day windows for input/output/total
397	     tokens and cost; `unknown` rendered as `—` not `0`
398	   - Burn estimate row: daily token rate, subscription burn class, confidence
399	     label (`high` / `medium` / `low`), freshness timestamp
400	   - Performance signals: p50/p95 latency, success rate, sample count, window
401	   - Signal source provenance list: which sources contributed to this snapshot
402	     (e.g. `stats-cache`, `native-session-jsonl`, `ddx-metrics`, `none`)
403	   - Data fetched from `GET /api/providers/:harness` on panel open
404	
405	   **Unknown-state semantics:**
406	   - `unknown` values are rendered with an explicit `—` or "unknown" label
407	     and a tooltip explaining why (e.g., "no stable non-PTY quota source
408	     confirmed for Claude")
409	   - Fields from provider-native sources are labeled "provider-reported"
410	   - Fields derived by DDx from observed metrics are labeled "DDx-estimated"
411	   - Fields that are genuinely absent carry a `?` badge, not a synthesized `0`
412	
413	   **Relationship to other views:**
414	   - Provider dashboard is host+user global (not project-scoped); it is
415	     accessible from the top-level navigation bar alongside the project picker
416	   - The status dashboard worker cards link to worker detail and the agent log
417	     monitor — not to the provider dashboard
418	   - The agent log monitor session detail links to the provider dashboard for
419	     the harness used in that invocation (via harness name)
420	   - The provider dashboard does NOT embed worker progress, bead state, or
421	     execution history; those live in their own dedicated views
422	
423	   **Reporting and export:**
424	   - "Copy JSON" action on the detail panel copies the `GET /api/providers/:harness`
425	     response verbatim — useful for bug reports and operator audits
426	   - Time-window selector (7d / 30d / custom) scopes the historical usage table
427	
428	9. **Persona viewer**
429	   - Browse personas with descriptions and tags
430	   - See which roles each persona is bound to in project config
431	   - View full persona content rendered as markdown
432	
433	### Performance Contract: Artifact Listing
434	
435	The artifact listing view carries a provisional performance contract for design
436	work. These budgets shape the current implementation, but the gating numbers
437	stay provisional until the B7-C1 baseline measurement is recorded and published
438	in the perf docs. Treat the values below as design-time budgets, not final
439	thresholds.
440	
441	| Budget | Provisional contract | Revisit when |
442	|---|---|---|
443	| Initial load | First usable content within 2s on the reference fixture | Baseline measurement lands with different gating numbers |
444	| Search/filter latency | Interactions settle within 200ms once the current list data is already loaded | Search becomes data-loading bound instead of DOM-bound |
445	| Steady-state DOM rows | Default list view stays flat and non-virtualized at up to 500 visible artifact rows | The default state needs 500-1000 visible rows, more than 1000 visible rows, or sticky group headers |
446	
447	- Revisit the contract before shipping if the artifact listing design adds any
448	  of the following:
449	  - infinite scroll
450	  - auto-prefetch
451	  - 500-1000 visible DOM rows in the default list state
452	  - more than 1000 visible DOM rows in the default list state
453	  - sticky group headers
454	- The default list view assumes a flat, non-virtualized DOM. If the design
455	  changes that assumption, update this section and the TP-002 measurement
456	  methodology together.
457	- Story 5 is page-local unless we explicitly add server-side `groupBy`: the
458	  browser groups only the loaded artifact edges. Story 5 owners should treat
459	  any requirement for cross-page or corpus-wide grouping accuracy as the
460	  trigger to move `groupBy` into the GraphQL resolver and make it server-side.
461	- Story 6 search/filter stays server-side: any query that changes result
462	  membership (`q`, `mediaType`, `staleness`, `phase`, `prefix`, `sort`) must
463	  continue to round-trip through the resolver so pagination and bookmarks stay
464	  correct. Story 6 owners should treat server-side filtering as mandatory, not
465	  optional, for any corpus-wide search or filter UI.
466	- Add server-side `groupBy` when group headers must reflect the full corpus,
467	  grouping needs to stay correct across page boundaries, or the page-local
468	  grouping pass can no longer represent the loaded set faithfully.
469	- Add virtualization when the visible artifact list needs more than the flat
470	  500-row DOM budget, or when scroll cost is dominated by DOM rendering rather
471	  than data loading.
472	
473	### Non-Functional
474	
475	- **Performance:** Initial page load <2s. API calls <200ms. Graph renders smoothly for 100+ nodes.
476	- **Deployment:** Single binary. No external runtime dependencies. No separate frontend server in production.
477	- **Development:** `bun run dev` proxies to Go server. Hot reload for frontend. `go run` for backend.
478	- **Responsive:** Usable on tablet/laptop screens. Not optimized for mobile.
479

481	
482	### US-080: Developer Browses Document Library
483	**As a** developer onboarding to a project
484	**I want** to open a web UI and browse all available documents
485	**So that** I can quickly understand what documents exist and how they're organized
486	
487	**Acceptance Criteria:**
488	- Given ddx-server is running, when I open `http://localhost:PORT`, then I am redirected to `/nodes/:nodeId` and see a dashboard with document/artifact counts by category
489	- Given I click on "Personas", then I see a list of all persona documents with names and descriptions
490	- Given I click on a persona, then I see its full content rendered as markdown
491	- Given I press the browser Back button, then I return to the Personas list with my scroll position preserved
492	
493	**E2E Test:** `artifacts.spec.ts` — full workflow: land on dashboard → navigate to personas → open persona → verify rendered content → navigate back
494	
495	### US-081: Developer Views and Navigates Dependency Graph
496	**As a** developer checking document health
497	**I want** to see a visual graph of document dependencies I can interact with
498	**So that** I can identify stale documents, understand relationships, and navigate directly to any doc from the graph
499	
500	**Acceptance Criteria:**
501	- Given I open the graph view, then I see nodes and edges representing dependency relationships; the graph renders with correct dimensions on first paint (no collapsed/zero-size SVG)
502	- Given documents are stale, then stale nodes are colored amber; documents with missing dependencies are colored red; fresh documents are green
503	- Given I click a node, then I navigate to that document's artifact detail view without a full page reload
504	- Given I press Back after clicking a node, then I return to the graph with the same zoom level and pan position restored
505	- Given I pan or zoom the graph, then the viewport state persists across the Back/Forward navigation cycle
506	- Given I apply a staleness filter chip (fresh / stale / missing), then only matching nodes and their connecting edges remain visible
507	- Given I apply a type filter chip, then only nodes of that media type remain visible
508	- Given I am on the graph page, then a `View documents` link is visible that navigates to the artifact browser filtered to markdown
509	- Given I am on an artifact detail page, then a `View in Graph` link navigates to the graph with that node highlighted
510	- Given the graph is rendered in either light or dark theme, then every document graph edge stroke and arrowhead maintains WCAG AA non-text contrast (>=3:1 against the canvas background) in both themes
511	
512	**E2E Test:** `graph.spec.ts` — full workflow: open graph → verify non-zero render → pan and zoom → click stale node → verify amber color → click node → verify artifact detail → Back → verify same viewport → apply staleness filter → verify node count changes → follow `View documents` link
513	
514	### US-081b: Developer Views and Regenerates Multi-Media Artifacts
515	**As a** developer working with generated project artifacts
516	**I want** the artifact browser to list all artifacts by type and render each
517	  one by media type, exposing regeneration when provenance exists
518	**So that** diagrams, images, PDFs, and markdown docs are all inspectable and
519	  refreshable without leaving the UI
520	
521	**Acceptance Criteria:**
522	- Given I navigate to `/nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/artifacts`, then I see
523	  a list of all project artifacts with category filter chips and a search bar
524	- Given I filter by category (e.g. "images"), then only artifacts of that
525	  media type are shown and the URL updates to reflect the filter
526	- Given an artifact has `media_type: text/markdown`, when I open it, then I see
527	  rendered markdown with syntax-highlighted code blocks
528	- Given an artifact has Mermaid-generated SVG metadata, Excalidraw JSON,
529	  `image/*`, or `application/pdf`, when I open it, then the viewer renders the
530	  corresponding preview and still exposes the raw sidecar metadata
531	- Given an artifact's media type is unknown or binary-only, then the viewer
532	  shows an explicit metadata-only fallback with an open/download affordance
533	- Given an artifact has `generated_by`, then I see the producing run id,

541	- Given I navigate to `/documents`, then I am redirected to `/artifacts` with a

544	**E2E Test:** `artifacts.spec.ts` — full workflow: open artifacts list → filter by category → open artifact → verify media renderer → open provenance panel → click Regenerate → verify run link → follow link to run detail → navigate back to artifact → navigate back to list

1165	## Provider Dashboard: Playwright Fixture Scenarios
1166	
1167	The provider dashboard has deterministic fixture data requirements so that
1168	Playwright tests can cover all meaningful display states without live provider
1169	credentials. The fixture layer is a static JSON handler mounted at
1170	`/api/providers` and `/api/providers/:harness` during test runs. Each scenario
1171	below defines the fixture variant and the expected rendered state.
1172	
1173	### Fixture Scenarios
1174	
1175	**Scenario 1 — all-healthy (green path)**
1176	
1177	Provider list returns two harnesses, both `available` + `authenticated` +
1178	`quota_headroom: ok`. Detail panel shows populated 7d/30d usage, burn estimate
1179	with `confidence: high`, and `signal_sources: ["native-session-jsonl", "ddx-metrics"]`.
1180	Expected: all badges green, no `?` badges, no unknown labels.
1181	
1182	```json
1183	[
1184	  {
1185	    "harness": "codex", "status": "available", "auth_state": "authenticated",
1186	    "quota_headroom": "ok", "cost_class": "subscription",
1187	    "signal_sources": ["native-session-jsonl", "ddx-metrics"],
1188	    "freshness_ts": "2026-04-14T05:00:00Z",
1189	    "recent_success_rate": 0.99, "recent_latency_p50_ms": 3100
1190	  },
1191	  {
1192	    "harness": "claude", "status": "available", "auth_state": "authenticated",
1193	    "quota_headroom": "ok", "cost_class": "subscription",
1194	    "signal_sources": ["stats-cache", "ddx-metrics"],
1195	    "freshness_ts": "2026-04-14T05:00:00Z",
1196	    "recent_success_rate": 0.97, "recent_latency_p50_ms": 4200
1197	  }
1198	]
1199	```
1200	
1201	**Scenario 2 — quota unknown (common real-world state for Claude)**
1202	
1203	Claude harness has `quota_headroom: unknown` because no stable non-PTY quota
1204	source exists. Codex is `quota_headroom: ok`. Expected: Claude row shows an
1205	"unknown" pill with tooltip "no stable non-PTY quota source confirmed". All
1206	other state fields are populated.
1207	
1208	**Scenario 3 — provider unavailable**
1209	
1210	One harness has `status: unavailable` and `auth_state: unauthenticated`.
1211	Expected: row shows red "unavailable" badge and gray "unauthenticated" badge.
1212	Detail panel shows empty usage tables with `—` cells and a signal source list
1213	of `["none"]`.
1214	
1215	**Scenario 4 — stale signals / low-confidence burn**
1216	
1217	Provider is available and authenticated, but `freshness_ts` is 48 hours ago
1218	and `burn_estimate.confidence` is `low`. Expected: freshness timestamp renders
1219	with a "stale" indicator; burn estimate row shows `confidence: low` label.
1220	
1221	**Scenario 5 — all unknown (offline / fresh install)**

1256	### Embedding Pattern (Pocketbase-style)
1257	
1258	```go
1259	//go:embed all:frontend/build
1260	var frontendFiles embed.FS
1261	
1262	func main() {
1263	    distFS, _ := fs.Sub(frontendFiles, "frontend/build") // relative to embed.go in cli/internal/server/
1264	    
1265	    mux := http.NewServeMux()
1266	    mux.Handle("/api/", apiRouter)
1267	    mux.Handle("/mcp/", mcpHandler)
1268	    mux.Handle("/", spaHandler(http.FS(distFS))) // SPA fallback
1269	}
1270	
1271	// spaHandler serves static files, falling back to index.html for SPA routing
1272	func spaHandler(fs http.FileSystem) http.Handler {
1273	    fileServer := http.FileServer(fs)
1274	    return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
1275	        if _, err := fs.Open(r.URL.Path); err != nil {
1276	            r.URL.Path = "/"
1277	        }
1278	        fileServer.ServeHTTP(w, r)
1279	    })
1280	}
1281	```

208	  execDef: DocumentExecDef

210	  content: String

1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	// Bead represents a portable work item with metadata.
10	// The schema matches bd/br JSONL format for interchange compatibility.
11	// Unknown fields from external sources are preserved in Extra.
12	type Bead struct {
13		ID        string    `json:"id"`
14		Title     string    `json:"title"`
15		Status    string    `json:"status"`
16		Priority  int       `json:"priority"`
17		IssueType string    `json:"issue_type"`
18		Owner     string    `json:"owner,omitempty"`
19		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
20		CreatedBy string    `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
21		UpdatedAt time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
22	
23		// Optional fields (bd-compatible)
24		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
25		Parent      string   `json:"parent,omitempty"`
26		Description string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
27		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
28		Notes       string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
29	
30		// Dependencies use bd-compatible format
31		Dependencies []Dependency `json:"dependencies,omitempty"`
32	
33		// Extra holds unknown fields for round-trip preservation.

78	// CanonicalStatuses is the single source of truth for the persisted bead

81	// cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json. The CI guard tests in
82	// sync_test.go assert that schema, TD doc, and Go source agree with this
83	// list; adding or removing a value here without updating the schema and TD
84	// will fail CI.
85	var CanonicalStatuses = []string{

90		StatusProposed,
91		StatusCancelled,

94	// IsCanonicalStatus reports whether s is one of the persisted bead statuses
95	// in CanonicalStatuses.
96	func IsCanonicalStatus(s string) bool {
97		for _, c := range CanonicalStatuses {
98			if c == s {
99				return true
100			}
101		}
102		return false

105	// IssueType constants
106	const (
107		// IssueTypeOperatorPrompt is the bead type used for operator-submitted
108		// prompts that the execute-loop runs as instructions.
109		IssueTypeOperatorPrompt = "operator-prompt"
110	)
111	
112	// Default labels and other defaults for operator-prompt beads.
113	const (
114		OperatorPromptLabelKind   = "kind:operator-prompt"
115		OperatorPromptLabelSource = "source:web-ui"
116		// OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance is the auto-AC stub used when an
117		// operator submits a prompt without explicit acceptance criteria. The
118		// structural AC verifier is skipped for operator-prompt beads, so this
119		// stub stands as a human-readable contract only.
120		OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance = "Agent must produce a diff or no_changes rationale; the prompt body is the contract."
121	)
122	
123	// OperatorPromptMutationGuard enforces the no-self-mutation rule from
124	// Story 15: an operator-prompt bead's execution may not create, edit, or
125	// close another operator-prompt bead. The guard returns nil when the
126	// mutation is allowed and a non-nil error when it must be rejected.
127	//
128	// actorIssueType is the issue_type of the bead currently being executed
129	// (empty when no operator-prompt context is active). targetIssueType is
130	// the issue_type of the bead about to be mutated.
131	//
132	// Allow/deny matrix:
133	//
134	//	actor=""               , target=*                → allow
135	//	actor="task"           , target=*                → allow
136	//	actor="operator-prompt", target!="operator-prompt" → allow
137	//	actor="operator-prompt", target=="operator-prompt" → deny
138	func OperatorPromptMutationGuard(actorIssueType, targetIssueType string) error {
139		if actorIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
140			return nil
141		}
142		if targetIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
143			return nil
144		}
145		return fmt.Errorf("bead: operator-prompt bead may not mutate another operator-prompt bead")
146	}
147	
148	// NewOperatorPromptBead constructs a fresh operator-prompt bead from a raw
149	// prompt string, applying the Story 15 template defaults: title is the first
150	// non-empty line of the prompt, full prompt body is preserved verbatim in the
151	// description, default labels are kind:operator-prompt + source:web-ui, the
152	// status starts in `proposed` (approval flow), priority defaults to the
153	// caller-supplied tier (clamped to MinPriority..MaxPriority), and the
154	// acceptance field carries the auto-AC stub.
155	//
156	// The returned bead is not persisted; callers feed it to Store.Create which
157	// will assign the ID and CreatedAt/UpdatedAt timestamps.
158	func NewOperatorPromptBead(prompt string, defaultTier int) *Bead {
159		body := strings.TrimSpace(prompt)
160		title := body
161		if i := strings.IndexByte(body, '\n'); i >= 0 {
162			title = strings.TrimSpace(body[:i])
163		}
164		if title == "" {
165			title = "(empty operator prompt)"
166		}
167		tier := defaultTier
168		if tier < MinPriority {
169			tier = MinPriority
170		}
171		if tier > MaxPriority {
172			tier = MaxPriority
173		}
174		return &Bead{
175			Title:       title,
176			IssueType:   IssueTypeOperatorPrompt,
177			Status:      StatusProposed,
178			Priority:    tier,
179			Labels:      []string{OperatorPromptLabelKind, OperatorPromptLabelSource},
180			Description: body,
181			Acceptance:  OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance,
182		}
183	}
184	
185	// Default values
186	const (
187		DefaultType     = "task"
188		DefaultStatus   = StatusOpen
189		DefaultPriority = 2
190		DefaultPrefix   = "bx" // used only when repo name detection fails
191		MinPriority     = 0
192		MaxPriority     = 4
193	)

234	// DepIDs returns a flat list of dependency IDs for this bead.
235	func (b *Bead) DepIDs() []string {
236		var ids []string
237		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
238			ids = append(ids, d.DependsOnID)
239		}
240		return ids
241	}
242	
243	// HasDep returns true if the bead depends on the given ID.
244	func (b *Bead) HasDep(id string) bool {
245		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
246			if d.DependsOnID == id {
247				return true
248			}
249		}
250		return false
251	}
252	
253	// AddDep adds a dependency if it doesn't already exist.
254	func (b *Bead) AddDep(depID, depType string) {
255		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
256			if d.DependsOnID == depID {
257				return // already exists
258			}
259		}
260		b.Dependencies = append(b.Dependencies, Dependency{
261			IssueID:     b.ID,
262			DependsOnID: depID,
263			Type:        depType,
264			CreatedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
265		})
266	}
267	
268	// RemoveDep removes a dependency by target ID.
269	func (b *Bead) RemoveDep(depID string) {
270		var filtered []Dependency
271		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
272			if d.DependsOnID != depID {
273				filtered = append(filtered, d)
274			}
275		}
276		b.Dependencies = filtered
277	}

22	// reviewFailureRunner is a test-local deterministic Executor + Reviewer pair
23	// driving the "N reviewer failures, then 1 success" scenario. Test-local on
24	// purpose: a shared cross-package testfixtures package would be unreachable
25	// from main() under deadcode RTA (production-reachability check).
26	type reviewFailureRunner struct {
27		resultRev     string
28		failUntilCall int
29		reviewCalls   atomic.Int32
30		execCalls     atomic.Int32
31	}
32	
33	func (r *reviewFailureRunner) Executor() agent.ExecuteBeadExecutor {
34		return agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
35			n := r.execCalls.Add(1)
36			return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
37				BeadID:    beadID,
38				Status:    agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
39				SessionID: fmt.Sprintf("rfr-sess-%d", n),
40				ResultRev: r.resultRev,
41			}, nil
42		})
43	}
44	
45	func (r *reviewFailureRunner) Reviewer() agent.BeadReviewer {
46		return reviewerFn(func(_ context.Context, _, resultRev string, _ agent.ImplementerRouting) (*agent.ReviewResult, error) {
47			n := int(r.reviewCalls.Add(1))
48			if n <= r.failUntilCall {
49				class := evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
50				return &agent.ReviewResult{
51						Verdict:   agent.VerdictBlock,
52						Error:     class,
53						ResultRev: resultRev,
54					}, fmt.Errorf("review-failure-runner: %s: %w", class,
55						errors.New("simulated reviewer failure"))
56			}
57			return &agent.ReviewResult{
58				Verdict:   agent.VerdictApprove,
59				Rationale: "review-failure-runner: APPROVE",
60				PerAC: []agent.ReviewAC{
61					{Number: 1, Item: "retry threshold", Grade: "pass", Evidence: "APPROVE after FailUntilCall"},
62				},
63				ResultRev: resultRev,
64			}, nil
65		})
66	}
67	
68	func (r *reviewFailureRunner) ReviewCalls() int { return int(r.reviewCalls.Load()) }
69	func (r *reviewFailureRunner) ExecCalls() int   { return int(r.execCalls.Load()) }
70	
71	type reviewerFn func(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl agent.ImplementerRouting) (*agent.ReviewResult, error)
72	
73	func (f reviewerFn) ReviewBead(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl agent.ImplementerRouting) (*agent.ReviewResult, error) {
74		return f(ctx, beadID, resultRev, impl)
75	}

5	// OperatorCancelReason marks a landing preserved because the operator
6	// cancelled the attempt mid-flight via /api/beads/<id>/cancel. ADR-022
7	// §Cancel SLA: the worker aborts at the next safe point (between LLM turns /
8	// git ops) and emits a preserved_for_review result with this exact reason.
9	const OperatorCancelReason = "operator_cancel"
10	
11	// PushFailedReasonPrefix is the canonical Reason prefix written by the
12	// landing layer when `git push` rejects the new tip after a local merge or
13	// fast-forward succeeded. ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus matches on this prefix
14	// to map a merged-locally-but-not-pushed outcome to push_failed instead of
15	// success, so the bead stays open.
16	const PushFailedReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push failed:"
17	
18	// PushConflictReasonPrefix marks a more specific failure mode than
19	// PushFailedReasonPrefix: the local push was rejected because the remote
20	// advanced, the loop attempted an automatic pull/merge to recover, and the
21	// merge produced a conflict the loop cannot resolve. The bead is parked for
22	// human review with a short cooldown rather than a year-long park.
23	const PushConflictReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push conflict:"
24	
25	// FailureMode constants classify *why* an execution did not land cleanly.
26	// They are orthogonal to Outcome/Status: a single failed attempt carries one
27	// outcome (e.g. preserved) and one failure mode (e.g. merge_conflict) so
28	// measurement surfaces can aggregate failures by cause.

30		FailureModeContextOverflow                = "context_overflow"

44		FailureModeUnknown                        = "unknown"
45	)

47	// ResourceExhaustedStopMessage is the operator-visible message emitted when
48	// execution must stop because the host cannot safely continue draining the
49	// queue.
50	const ResourceExhaustedStopMessage = "resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop"
51	
52	// ClassifyFailureMode derives a failure_mode for a worker-level result from
53	// its outcome, exit code, and error message. Returns "" when the outcome is
54	// task_succeeded (success carries no failure mode). task_no_changes always
55	// maps to FailureModeNoChanges regardless of error text.
56	//
57	// For task_failed (or non-zero exit code), the error message is scanned for
58	// well-known substrings. The order matters: context/auth/timeout patterns
59	// are checked before generic test/build patterns so a "test timed out"
60	// classifies as timeout rather than test_failure. Anything unrecognised
61	// maps to FailureModeUnknown so aggregates never hide a missed pattern.
62	func ClassifyFailureMode(outcome string, exitCode int, errMsg string) string {
63		switch outcome {
64		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
65			if exitCode == 0 {
66				return ""
67			}
68			// Degenerate: success outcome with non-zero exit. Treat as unknown
69			// failure rather than success so the aggregate flags the anomaly.
70		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
71			return FailureModeNoChanges
72		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
73			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
74		}
75	
76		lower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)
77		switch {
78		case IsLockContentionError(errMsg):
79			return FailureModeLockContention
80		case containsAny(lower,
81			"passthrough constraint unsatisfiable",
82			"passthrough constraint:",
83			"max_power is less than min_power",
84			"max_power exceeds min_power",
85			"harness cannot satisfy power constraint",
86			"harness pin incompatible with power bounds",
87			"model pin incompatible with power bounds",
88			"provider pin incompatible with power bounds"):
89			return FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint
90		case containsAny(lower,

92			"no model meets min_power",
93			"no model with power >=",
94			"minimum power not achievable",
95			"min_power constraint cannot be satisfied"):
96			return FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied
97		case containsAny(lower,
98			"executable file not found in $path",
99			"executable file not found",
100			"no such file or directory") &&
101			containsAny(lower, "exec:", "harness", "claude", "agent", "codex", "gemini"):
102			return FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled
103		case containsAny(lower,
104			"no viable harness",
105			"no harness configured",
106			"failed to initialize routing service",
107			"no viable provider"):
108			return FailureModeNoViableProvider
109		case containsAny(lower,
110			"context length",
111			"context_length_exceeded",
112			"context window",
113			"maximum context",
114			"prompt is too long",
115			"prompt too long",
116			"token limit",
117			"too many tokens",
118			"exceeds the context"):
119			return FailureModeContextOverflow

1	// Package triage provides the post-attempt decision module that the
2	// drain loop consults after each terminal event. Given a bead's failure
3	// mode and the prior modes seen in this bead's attempt history, the
4	// policy returns the next action to take (re-attempt, escalate, file
5	// follow-up, etc.).
6	//
7	// This package is the standalone policy core; loop integration lands in
8	// a follow-up bead.

10	
11	import "fmt"
12	
13	// FailureMode classifies the terminal event of a single execute-bead attempt.
14	type FailureMode string
15	
16	const (
17		// FailureModeReviewBlock — reviewer returned BLOCK on the committed result.
18		FailureModeReviewBlock FailureMode = "review_block"
19		// FailureModeLockContention — claim/lock could not be acquired.
20		FailureModeLockContention FailureMode = "lock_contention"
21		// FailureModeExecutionFailed — runner crashed, harness errored, or
22		// timed out without producing a usable commit.
23		FailureModeExecutionFailed FailureMode = "execution_failed"
24		// FailureModeNoChanges — attempt completed without producing a commit.
25		FailureModeNoChanges FailureMode = "no_changes"
26	)
27	
28	// Action is the decision the policy returns for an attempt.
29	type Action string
30	
31	const (
32		// ActionReAttemptWithContext — re-queue the bead with the prior
33		// failure context threaded into the next prompt.
34		ActionReAttemptWithContext Action = "re_attempt_with_context"
35		// ActionEscalateTier — re-queue with a higher-capability model tier.
36		ActionEscalateTier Action = "escalate_tier"
37		// ActionRetryWithBackoff — retry after a delay (lock contention etc.).
38		ActionRetryWithBackoff Action = "retry_with_backoff"
39		// ActionFileFollowup — file a follow-up bead and close this one.
40		ActionFileFollowup Action = "file_followup"
41		// ActionNeedsHuman — park the bead for manual triage.
42		ActionNeedsHuman Action = "needs_human"
43	)
44	
45	// TriagePolicy maps a FailureMode to an ordered ladder of Actions. The
46	// nth time a given mode is seen in a bead's history, the nth rung of
47	// its ladder is returned. Past the end of the ladder, the final rung
48	// (typically ActionNeedsHuman) is returned.
49	type TriagePolicy struct {
50		Ladders map[FailureMode][]Action
51	}
52	
53	// DefaultPolicy returns the built-in policy. Mirrors the parent bead's
54	// AC#3: BLOCK → re_attempt_with_context, escalate_tier, needs_human.
55	func DefaultPolicy() TriagePolicy {
56		return TriagePolicy{Ladders: map[FailureMode][]Action{
57			FailureModeReviewBlock: {
58				ActionReAttemptWithContext,
59				ActionEscalateTier,
60				ActionNeedsHuman,
61			},
62			FailureModeLockContention: {
63				ActionRetryWithBackoff,
64				ActionRetryWithBackoff,
65				ActionNeedsHuman,
66			},
67			FailureModeExecutionFailed: {
68				ActionReAttemptWithContext,
69				ActionEscalateTier,
70				ActionNeedsHuman,
71			},
72			FailureModeNoChanges: {
73				ActionReAttemptWithContext,
74				ActionFileFollowup,
75				ActionNeedsHuman,
76			},
77		}}
78	}
79	
80	// Decide returns the next Action for an attempt that ended in `mode`,
81	// given the ordered list of failure modes already seen for this bead
82	// (most recent last; the current attempt is NOT included).
83	//
84	// beadID is accepted for future per-bead policy customisation and to
85	// keep call sites stable; the default policy ignores it.
86	func (p TriagePolicy) Decide(beadID string, mode FailureMode, history []FailureMode) Action {
87		ladder := p.Ladders[mode]
88		if len(ladder) == 0 {
89			return ActionNeedsHuman
90		}
91		n := 0
92		for _, m := range history {
93			if m == mode {
94				n++
95			}
96		}
97		if n >= len(ladder) {
98			return ladder[len(ladder)-1]
99		}
100		return ladder[n]
101	}
102	
103	// ParseAction validates and converts a string from config into an Action.
104	func ParseAction(s string) (Action, error) {
105		switch Action(s) {
106		case ActionReAttemptWithContext,
107			ActionEscalateTier,
108			ActionRetryWithBackoff,
109			ActionFileFollowup,
110			ActionNeedsHuman:
111			return Action(s), nil
112		default:
113			return "", fmt.Errorf("triage: unknown action %q", s)
114		}
115	}
116	
117	// ParseFailureMode validates and converts a string from config into a FailureMode.
118	func ParseFailureMode(s string) (FailureMode, error) {
119		switch FailureMode(s) {
120		case FailureModeReviewBlock,
121			FailureModeLockContention,
122			FailureModeExecutionFailed,
123			FailureModeNoChanges:
124			return FailureMode(s), nil
125		default:
126			return "", fmt.Errorf("triage: unknown failure mode %q", s)
127		}
128	}

```go
func (s *Store) Status() (*StatusCounts, error) {
	beads, err := s.ReadAll()
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	ready, err := s.Ready()
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	blocked, err := s.Blocked()
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}

	// Pull in archive partner so totals survive `bead migrate` — the archive
	// only carries closed beads, so Ready/Blocked aren't affected.
	seen := make(map[string]bool, len(beads))
	all := make([]Bead, 0, len(beads))
	for _, b := range beads {
		if seen[b.ID] {
			continue
		}
		seen[b.ID] = true
		all = append(all, b)
	}
	if s.Collection == DefaultCollection {
		archive := s.archivePartner()
		if archived, aerr := archive.ReadAll(); aerr == nil {
			for _, b := range archived {
				if seen[b.ID] {
					continue
				}
				seen[b.ID] = true
				all = append(all, b)
			}
		}
	}

	counts := &StatusCounts{Total: len(all), Ready: len(ready), Blocked: len(blocked)}
	for _, b := range all {
		switch b.Status {
		case StatusOpen:
			counts.Open++
		case StatusClosed:
			counts.Closed++
		}
	}
	return counts, nil
}
```

54	// AC#3: BLOCK → re_attempt_with_context, escalate_tier, needs_human.
55	func DefaultPolicy() TriagePolicy {
56		return TriagePolicy{Ladders: map[FailureMode][]Action{
57			FailureModeReviewBlock: {
58				ActionReAttemptWithContext,
59				ActionEscalateTier,
60				ActionNeedsHuman,
61			},
62			FailureModeLockContention: {
63				ActionRetryWithBackoff,
64				ActionRetryWithBackoff,
65				ActionNeedsHuman,
66			},
67			FailureModeExecutionFailed: {
68				ActionReAttemptWithContext,
69				ActionEscalateTier,
70				ActionNeedsHuman,
71			},
72			FailureModeNoChanges: {
73				ActionReAttemptWithContext,
74				ActionFileFollowup,
75				ActionNeedsHuman,
76			},
77		}}
78	}
79	
80	// Decide returns the next Action for an attempt that ended in `mode`,
81	// given the ordered list of failure modes already seen for this bead
82	// (most recent last; the current attempt is NOT included).
83	//
84	// beadID is accepted for future per-bead policy customisation and to
85	// keep call sites stable; the default policy ignores it.
86	func (p TriagePolicy) Decide(beadID string, mode FailureMode, history []FailureMode) Action {
87		ladder := p.Ladders[mode]
88		if len(ladder) == 0 {
89			return ActionNeedsHuman
90		}
91		n := 0
92		for _, m := range history {
93			if m == mode {
94				n++
95			}
96		}
97		if n >= len(ladder) {
98			return ladder[len(ladder)-1]
99		}
100		return ladder[n]
101	}
102	
103	// ParseAction validates and converts a string from config into an Action.
104	func ParseAction(s string) (Action, error) {
105		switch Action(s) {
106		case ActionReAttemptWithContext,
107			ActionEscalateTier,
108			ActionRetryWithBackoff,
109			ActionFileFollowup,
110			ActionNeedsHuman:
111			return Action(s), nil
112		default:
113			return "", fmt.Errorf("triage: unknown action %q", s)
114		}
115	}
116	
117	// ParseFailureMode validates and converts a string from config into a FailureMode.
118	func ParseFailureMode(s string) (FailureMode, error) {
119		switch FailureMode(s) {
120		case FailureModeReviewBlock,
121			FailureModeLockContention,
122			FailureModeExecutionFailed,
123			FailureModeNoChanges:
124			return FailureMode(s), nil
125		default:
126			return "", fmt.Errorf("triage: unknown failure mode %q", s)
127		}
128	}

10	func TestEventsByKind(t *testing.T) {
11		s := newTestStore(t)
12	
13		b := &Bead{Title: "test bead"}
14		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
15	
16		// Append a mix of event kinds.
17		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "routing", Summary: "provider=claude model=opus reason=config"}))
18		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "summary", Summary: "completed"}))
19		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "routing", Summary: "provider=claude model=sonnet reason=fallback"}))
20		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "error", Summary: "transient failure"}))
21		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "routing", Summary: "provider=local model=qwen reason=cost"}))
22	
23		routing, err := s.EventsByKind(b.ID, "routing")
24		require.NoError(t, err)
25		require.Len(t, routing, 3)
26		assert.Equal(t, "provider=claude model=opus reason=config", routing[0].Summary)
27		assert.Equal(t, "provider=claude model=sonnet reason=fallback", routing[1].Summary)
28		assert.Equal(t, "provider=local model=qwen reason=cost", routing[2].Summary)
29	
30		summaries, err := s.EventsByKind(b.ID, "summary")
31		require.NoError(t, err)
32		require.Len(t, summaries, 1)
33		assert.Equal(t, "completed", summaries[0].Summary)
34	
35		missing, err := s.EventsByKind(b.ID, "nonexistent")
36		require.NoError(t, err)
37		assert.Empty(t, missing)
38	}

13	// seedBlocks appends `n` review-BLOCK events spaced 1 minute apart, starting
14	// at base. Returns the timestamp of the last BLOCK so test code can layer
15	// pairing-degraded events around it.
16	func seedBlocks(t *testing.T, store *bead.Store, beadID string, base time.Time, n int) time.Time {
17		t.Helper()
18		last := base
19		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
20			last = base.Add(time.Duration(i) * time.Minute)
21			require.NoError(t, store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
22				Kind:      "review",
23				Summary:   "BLOCK",
24				Body:      "rationale",
25				Actor:     "reviewer",
26				Source:    "test",
27				CreatedAt: last,
28			}))
29		}
30		return last
31	}
32	
33	// findEvent returns the last event of kind `kind` for bead, or nil.
34	func findEvent(t *testing.T, store *bead.Store, beadID, kind string) *bead.BeadEvent {
35		t.Helper()
36		events, err := store.Events(beadID)
37		require.NoError(t, err)
38		for i := len(events) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
39			if events[i].Kind == kind {
40				ev := events[i]
41				return &ev
42			}
43		}
44		return nil
45	}

56	// TestApplyReviewTriageDecision_FirstBlockReAttempts verifies that the first
57	// BLOCK on a bead chooses re_attempt_with_context: no tier hint is set, no
58	// needs_human label is added, and a triage-decision event records the action.
59	func TestApplyReviewTriageDecision_FirstBlockReAttempts(t *testing.T) {
60		store, b := newTriageTestStore(t)
61		now := time.Date(2026, 5, 2, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
62		seedBlocks(t, store, b.ID, now, 1)
63	
64		require.NoError(t, applyReviewTriageDecision(store, b.ID, "ddx", now.Add(time.Second), ""))
65	
66		got, err := store.Get(b.ID)
67		require.NoError(t, err)
68		assert.NotContains(t, got.Labels, TriageNeedsHumanLabel)
69		if got.Extra != nil {
70			_, hasHint := got.Extra[TriageTierHintKey]
71			assert.False(t, hasHint, "first BLOCK must not set tier hint")
72		}
73		ev := findEvent(t, store, b.ID, "triage-decision")
74		require.NotNil(t, ev)
75		assert.Contains(t, ev.Summary, "re_attempt_with_context")
76	}

117	// TestApplyReviewTriageDecision_PairingDegradedBiasesToReAttempt verifies that
118	// when the latest BLOCK was paired with a kind:review-pairing-degraded event
119	// from the same attempt window, the policy's escalate_tier rung is overridden
120	// to re_attempt_with_context so a freshly-paired reviewer gets another chance.
121	func TestApplyReviewTriageDecision_PairingDegradedBiasesToReAttempt(t *testing.T) {
122		store, b := newTriageTestStore(t)
123		now := time.Date(2026, 5, 2, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC)
124	
125		// First BLOCK at t0.
126		seedBlocks(t, store, b.ID, now, 1)
127		// Pairing-degraded event between BLOCK 1 and BLOCK 2.
128		require.NoError(t, store.AppendEvent(b.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
129			Kind:      ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind,
130			Summary:   "reviewer pinned to same provider",
131			Body:      "{}",
132			Source:    "test",
133			CreatedAt: now.Add(30 * time.Second),
134		}))
135		// Second BLOCK after pairing-degraded — same attempt window.
136		require.NoError(t, store.AppendEvent(b.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
137			Kind:      "review",
138			Summary:   "BLOCK",
139			Body:      "rationale",
140			Actor:     "reviewer",
141			Source:    "test",
142			CreatedAt: now.Add(time.Minute),
143		}))
144	
145		require.NoError(t, applyReviewTriageDecision(store, b.ID, "ddx", now.Add(time.Hour), string(escalation.TierStandard)))
146	
147		got, err := store.Get(b.ID)
148		require.NoError(t, err)
149		if got.Extra != nil {
150			_, hasHint := got.Extra[TriageTierHintKey]
151			assert.False(t, hasHint, "pairing-degraded must override escalate_tier; no tier hint expected")
152		}
153		assert.NotContains(t, got.Labels, TriageNeedsHumanLabel)
154		ev := findEvent(t, store, b.ID, "triage-decision")
155		require.NotNil(t, ev)
156		assert.Contains(t, ev.Summary, "re_attempt_with_context")
157		assert.Contains(t, ev.Body, "pairing_degraded")
158	}

22	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
23	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
24	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
25	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
26	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
27		Log             io.Writer
28		CleanupLog      io.Writer
29		CleanupRunner   executionCleanupRunner
30		CleanupInterval time.Duration
31		CleanupTickCh   <-chan time.Time
32		EventSink       io.Writer
33		ProgressCh      chan<- ProgressEvent
34		PreClaimHook    func(ctx context.Context) error
35		// PreClaimIntakeHook runs after routing preflight and before Claim. It
36		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only
37		// actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim. Non-atomic outcomes
38		// skip the bead without claim. intake_error fail-opens so infrastructure
39		// failures do not become hidden dispatch failures.
40		PreClaimIntakeHook    PreClaimIntakeHook
41		PreDispatchLintHook   func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
42		PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
43		// ReviewCostCap, when non-nil, accumulates reviewer cost on the same
44		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
45		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
46		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
47		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
48		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
49		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
50		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
51		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is
52		// claimed; DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list.
53		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
54		Once           bool
55		PollInterval   time.Duration
56		NoReview       bool
57		LabelFilter    string
58		SessionID      string
59		WorkerID       string
60		ProjectRoot    string
61		// TargetBeadID, when non-empty, restricts nextCandidate to only return the
62		// named bead from the execution-ready queue. Used by `ddx try <bead-id>`
63		// to dispatch a single specific bead through the same claim → executor →
64		// land path the queue drain uses. When empty, the picker behaves normally.
65		TargetBeadID string
66		// WakeCh, when non-nil, signals the idle-poll sleep to return immediately
67		// so the loop re-scans the queue. Used by the operator-prompt approve /
68		// auto-approve mutations (Story 15) to avoid a poll-interval-sized delay
69		// before a freshly-approved bead is claimed. Implementations must send
70		// non-blocking (server-side helpers do); the loop only waits for a
71		// receive on WakeCh during the idle sleep, never elsewhere.
72		WakeCh <-chan struct{}
73	}
74	
75	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
76	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
77	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
78	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
79	
80	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
81	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
82	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
83	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
84	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
85	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
86	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
87	
88	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
89	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
90	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
91	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
92	// advance the base branch.
93	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
94	
95	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
96	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
97	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
98	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
99	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
100	// worker.
101	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
102	
103	// PreClaimIntakeCooldown is the short retry pause applied when the
104	// model-backed readiness/intake hook itself fails. This is infrastructure
105	// noise, not an implementer attempt, so the loop must not claim the bead; the
106	// pause prevents multiple workers from hammering the same broken pre-claim
107	// path.
108	const PreClaimIntakeCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
109	
110	// ProviderUnavailableCooldown is the retry pause for route/provider outages
111	// where no configured tier can currently run the bead. This is transient
112	// scheduler state, not bead quality evidence, so it must not become a durable
113	// triage:needs-investigation park.
114	const ProviderUnavailableCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
115	
116	// SubmitWithPreMergeChecks is the canonical land-back step for the execute-bead
117	// loop. It runs the project's .ddx/checks/*.yaml gate against the worker's
118	// (baseRev, resultRev) before forwarding to submit. On Blocked, it preserves
119	// the worker's result under refs/ddx/iterations/, records checks-blocked
120	// events on the bead, and returns the synthesised LandResult{Status:"preserved"}
121	// without ever calling submit. On pass, honoured checks-bypass annotations are
122	// recorded as checks-bypass events and submit runs normally.
123	//
124	// submit is the project's land coordinator entry point (typically
125	// LocalLandCoordinator.Submit or the server-side coordinator submit).
126	//
127	// The implementation lives in checks_premerge.go alongside its supporting
128	// helpers (RunPreMergeChecks, AppendPreMergeChecksEvents,
129	// PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks); this thin wrapper exists so the execute-bead
130	// loop's land-back call site is one obvious helper rather than three nested
131	// ones, and so callers wiring the gate do not need to import the helper layer
132	// directly.
133	func SubmitWithPreMergeChecks(
134		ctx context.Context,
135		projectRoot string,
136		b *bead.Bead,
137		res *ExecuteBeadResult,
138		submit func(LandRequest) (*LandResult, error),
139		eventStore BeadEventAppender,
140		actor, source string,
141		now func() time.Time,
142	) (*LandResult, *PreMergeChecksOutcome, error) {
143		if now == nil {
144			now = time.Now
145		}
146		if submit == nil {
147			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("submit-with-pre-merge-checks: submit callback required")
148		}
149		evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir)
150		outcome, err := RunPreMergeChecks(ctx, projectRoot, b, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev, evidenceDir)
151		if err != nil {
152			// Treat checks_bypass / loader errors as a hard preserve: the worker
153			// did its job; the operator misconfigured the gate. Better to park
154			// the result under an iteration ref than to silently land it.
155			ref := PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
156			if upErr := (&RealOrchestratorGitOps{}).UpdateRef(projectRoot, ref, res.ResultRev); upErr != nil {
157				return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving result after checks setup error: %w (original error: %v)", upErr, err)
158			}
159			return &LandResult{
160				Status:      "preserved",
161				PreserveRef: ref,
162				Reason:      "pre-merge checks setup error: " + err.Error(),
163			}, nil, nil
164		}
165		if outcome.Blocked {
166			land, perr := PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks(projectRoot, res, outcome, &RealOrchestratorGitOps{})
167			if perr != nil {
168				return nil, outcome, perr
169			}
170			if eventStore != nil {
171				_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
172			}
173			return land, outcome, nil
174		}
175		if eventStore != nil && len(outcome.Bypassed) > 0 {
176			_ = AppendPreMergeChecksEvents(eventStore, res.BeadID, outcome, actor, source, now().UTC())
177		}
178		land, sErr := submit(BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, res))
179		return land, outcome, sErr
180	}
181	
182	// runPostAttemptTriage invokes the optional post-attempt triage hook after
183	// the attempt outcome has been finalized but before any cooldown decision is
184	// applied. Hook failures are fail-open: the report is returned unchanged and
185	// legacy cooldown behavior remains in force.
186	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) runPostAttemptTriage(ctx context.Context, candidate bead.Bead, report ExecuteBeadReport, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, now func() time.Time) ExecuteBeadReport {
187		hook := runtime.PostAttemptTriageHook
188		if hook == nil {
189			return report
190		}
191		triage, err := hook(ctx, candidate.ID, report)
192		if err != nil {
193			if runtime.Log != nil {
194				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, err)
195			}
196			return report
197		}
198		if strings.TrimSpace(triage.Classification) == "" {
199			return report
200		}
201		report.OutcomeReason = triage.Classification
202		recordPostAttemptTriageEvent(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, triage, assignee, now().UTC())
203		return report
204	}
205	
206	// CapLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
207	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
208	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
209	func CapLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
210		if d <= 0 {
211			return 0
212		}
213		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
214			return MaxLoopCooldown
215		}
216		return d
217	}

219	type ExecuteBeadReport struct {
220		BeadID    string `json:"bead_id"`
221		AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
222		WorkerID  string `json:"worker_id,omitempty"`
223		Harness   string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
224		Provider  string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
225		Model     string `json:"model,omitempty"`
226		// ActualPower is the routing-decision power of the model that actually
227		// served the implementer's call. Forwarded to the post-merge reviewer so
228		// it can request MinPower=actualPower+1 and bias routing toward a
229		// stronger reviewer (R4 pairing).
230		ActualPower                 int           `json:"actual_power,omitempty"`
231		PredictedPower              int           `json:"predicted_power,omitempty"`
232		PredictedSpeedTPS           float64       `json:"predicted_speed_tps,omitempty"`
233		PredictedCostUSDPer1kTokens float64       `json:"predicted_cost_usd_per_1k_tokens,omitempty"`
234		PredictedCostSource         string        `json:"predicted_cost_source,omitempty"`
235		Status                      string        `json:"status"`
236		Detail                      string        `json:"detail,omitempty"`
237		Error                       string        `json:"error,omitempty"`
238		Stderr                      string        `json:"stderr,omitempty"`
239		RateLimitBudget             time.Duration `json:"rate_limit_budget,omitempty"`
240		SessionID                   string        `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
241		BaseRev                     string        `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
242		ResultRev                   string        `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
243		PreserveRef                 string        `json:"preserve_ref,omitempty"`
244		RetryAfter                  string        `json:"retry_after,omitempty"`
245		// NoChangesRationale carries the agent's explanation when status == no_changes.
246		NoChangesRationale string `json:"no_changes_rationale,omitempty"`
247		// ReviewVerdict is the post-merge review verdict (APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES,
248		// or BLOCK) when a reviewer ran. Empty when review was skipped.
249		ReviewVerdict string `json:"review_verdict,omitempty"`
250		// ReviewRationale carries the actionable reviewer-authored findings for
251		// non-APPROVE review outcomes.
252		ReviewRationale string `json:"review_rationale,omitempty"`
253		// Tier is the model tier used for the final attempt (cheap, standard, smart).
254		// Populated by tier-escalating executors; empty for single-tier attempts.
255		Tier string `json:"tier,omitempty"`
256		// ProbeResult is a brief summary of the provider health probe at attempt time.
257		ProbeResult string `json:"probe_result,omitempty"`
258		// CostUSD is the dollar cost of this attempt as reported by the harness.
259		// Tier-escalating executors propagate this so the escalation trace can
260		// compute wasted/effective spend.
261		CostUSD float64 `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
262		// DurationMS is the wall-clock duration of this attempt.
263		DurationMS int64 `json:"duration_ms,omitempty"`
264		// Profile routing telemetry. Populated when execute-loop uses a profile
265		// ladder rather than an explicit harness/model pin.
266		RequestedProfile string `json:"requested_profile,omitempty"`
267		RequestedTier    string `json:"requested_tier,omitempty"`
268		ResolvedTier     string `json:"resolved_tier,omitempty"`
269		EscalationCount  int    `json:"escalation_count,omitempty"`
270		FinalTier        string `json:"final_tier,omitempty"`
271		// DecompositionRecommendation carries the structured list of recommended
272		// sub-bead titles when Status == declined_needs_decomposition. The loop
273		// records these on the bead as a `decomposition-recommendation` event so
274		// operators (or helix-evolve) can split the bead without re-deriving the
275		// recommendation.
276		DecompositionRecommendation []string `json:"decomposition_recommendation,omitempty"`
277		// DecompositionRationale is a free-form explanation accompanying
278		// DecompositionRecommendation. Optional.
279		DecompositionRationale string `json:"decomposition_rationale,omitempty"`
280		// Disrupted marks a failed attempt as worker-disrupted rather than
281		// model-gave-up: the model was prevented from making progress by an
282		// external cause (context cancellation, executor SIGKILL/SIGTERM,
283		// transport-class provider error, server restart, routing preflight
284		// rejection). The execute-loop bypasses the no-progress cooldown for
285		// Disrupted reports so the bead is immediately re-claimable instead of
286		// being parked for hours on a transient outage. ddx-5b3e57f4.
287		Disrupted bool `json:"disrupted,omitempty"`
288		// DisruptionReason carries the kind of disruption detected when
289		// Disrupted=true (e.g. "context_canceled", "context_deadline",
290		// "transport_error", "preflight_rejected"). Used in the
291		// `disruption_detected` event body so operators can see which class is
292		// occurring.
293		DisruptionReason string `json:"disruption_reason,omitempty"`
294		// OutcomeReason carries the machine-readable lifecycle classification for
295		// the attempt outcome. It complements Disrupted/DisruptionReason without
296		// changing their mechanical interruption semantics.
297		OutcomeReason string `json:"outcome_reason,omitempty"`
298		// ResourceExhausted carries the execution-root preflight result when the
299		// attempt stopped before the agent could safely continue.
300		ResourceExhausted any `json:"resource_exhausted,omitempty"`
301	}

## How needs_human Label is SET

The complete flow is in **execute_bead_post_review.go** (lines 443-482):

The `applyTriageAction()` function (lines 446-482) handles setting the needs_human label:

```go
// Case: triage.ActionNeedsHuman
case triage.ActionNeedsHuman:

        if !HasBeadLabel(b.Labels, TriageNeedsHumanLabel) {

```

23	func WithWorkingDir(ctx context.Context, workingDir string) context.Context {

53	// RunPostMergeReview executes the post-merge review state machine for a
54	// successful bead attempt. When review is skipped (no reviewer or --no-review)
55	// the bead is closed and the function returns Approved=true. When review is
56	// active, the execute-loop asks the review coordinator to evaluate the
57	// close-eligible result before CloseWithEvidence runs; close only proceeds on
58	// unanimous evidence-backed APPROVE. REQUEST_CHANGES and BLOCK record their
59	// review events without reopening the bead.

62	// review:skip-reason:* label; otherwise it is ignored. When review is not
63	// skipped, the review coordinator's verdict(s) or error drive the canonical
64	// event emissions (review / review-error / review-manual-required /
65	// review-malfunction), and on a non-APPROVE verdict the triage policy is
66	// still consulted without reopening the bead.

68	// Returns Approved=true on the APPROVE path and on the skip path; returns
69	// Approved=false on REQUEST_CHANGES, BLOCK, malformed APPROVE/BLOCK, or
